name: Louisiana SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
description: Louisiana's food assistance program providing monthly EBT benefits to purchase groceries. Administered by the Louisiana Department of Children and Family Services (DCFS).
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 130% FPL; net income at or below 100% FPL

name: Louisiana Medicaid (Healthy Louisiana)
description: Louisiana's Medicaid program providing health coverage for eligible low-income residents. Covers doctor visits, hospital care, prescriptions, mental health, and dental services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant women, elderly, and individuals with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults up to 138% FPL under Medicaid expansion; children up to 217% FPL

name: Louisiana Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary wage replacement benefits to workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Administered by the Louisiana Workforce Commission.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost employment through no fault of their own and meet wage/work requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned sufficient wages during the base period

name: Louisiana Housing Corporation
description: Provides affordable housing resources including rental assistance, homebuyer programs, homeless assistance, and housing rehabilitation for Louisiana residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income individuals and families seeking affordable housing
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally targets households at or below 80% AMI

name: Southeast Louisiana Legal Services (SLLS)
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income residents in southeast Louisiana including New Orleans, covering housing, family law, disaster recovery, and public benefits.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals at or below 125% of the federal poverty level in southeast Louisiana

name: Acadiana Legal Service Corporation
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income residents across central and northern Louisiana in matters including housing, family law, and consumer issues.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals in central and northern Louisiana parishes

name: Louisiana Workforce Commission - Job Centers
description: Louisiana's workforce development system offering job search assistance, skills training, resume help, and career counseling through American Job Centers statewide.
eligibilitySummary: All Louisiana residents seeking employment or career development services

name: Louisiana LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
description: Provides financial assistance to help low-income households pay heating and cooling bills. Administered by Louisiana Housing Corporation.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households with home energy costs, prioritizing elderly, disabled, and young children
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 150% FPL

name: Louisiana 211
description: Free, confidential referral service connecting Louisiana residents with local health and human services resources including food, housing, utilities, and crisis support.
eligibilitySummary: All Louisiana residents seeking assistance or referrals
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages via interpreter

name: Second Harvest Food Bank of Greater New Orleans and Acadiana
description: Feeding America affiliate serving 23 parishes in south Louisiana. Distributes food through partner agencies, food pantries, and community programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in the south Louisiana service area

name: Food Bank of Northwest Louisiana
description: Feeding America affiliate serving 12 parishes in northwest Louisiana. Provides food distribution and hunger relief programs throughout the Shreveport area and beyond.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in the northwest Louisiana service area

name: Food Bank of Northeast Louisiana
description: Feeding America affiliate serving parishes in northeast Louisiana including the Monroe area. Distributes food through partner agencies and community programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in the northeast Louisiana service area

name: Louisiana Department of Veterans Affairs
description: Assists Louisiana veterans and their families in obtaining federal and state benefits including healthcare, disability compensation, education, and housing.
eligibilitySummary: Veterans, active duty service members, and their dependents residing in Louisiana

name: Louisiana Crisis Line
description: Statewide crisis line providing immediate support for mental health emergencies, suicidal thoughts, and emotional distress for Louisiana residents.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Louisiana experiencing a mental health crisis

name: Louisiana Child Care Assistance Program (CCAP)
description: Provides financial assistance to help low-income families pay for childcare so parents can work, attend school, or participate in training programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income working families with children under age 13 who need childcare
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below 55% SMI initially

name: Louisiana FITAP (Family Independence Temporary Assistance Program)
description: Louisiana's TANF cash assistance program providing monthly benefits and supportive services to low-income families with children to achieve self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families with dependent children who meet work requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Very low income thresholds; varies by family size

name: Louisiana WIC Program
description: Women, Infants, and Children supplemental nutrition program providing healthy foods, nutrition education, and breastfeeding support for eligible participants.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children up to age 5

name: New Orleans Regional Transit Authority (RTA)
description: Public transit system serving the New Orleans metropolitan area with streetcar lines, bus routes, and ferry service.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all riders in the New Orleans metro area; reduced fares for seniors and disabled
hours: Varies by route; some routes operate 24/7

name: SporTran (Shreveport Transit)
description: Public transit system serving the Shreveport-Bossier City metropolitan area with fixed-route bus service and paratransit options.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all riders in the Shreveport-Bossier City area

name: Louisiana Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides energy efficiency improvements to low-income homes to reduce energy costs. Includes insulation, air sealing, and heating/cooling system upgrades.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters, with priority for elderly, disabled, and families with children
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% FPL

name: Louisiana Rehabilitation Services (LRS)
description: Provides vocational rehabilitation services for individuals with disabilities including job training, placement assistance, and supportive services.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with physical or mental disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: Housing Authority of New Orleans (HANO)
description: Provides affordable public housing and Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) for low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in the New Orleans area.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in New Orleans
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by family size; generally at or below 80% AMI for admission

name: Louisiana Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide hotline providing crisis intervention, safety planning, shelter referrals, and support for victims of domestic violence in Louisiana.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing or affected by domestic violence in Louisiana

name: TOPS (Taylor Opportunity Program for Students)
description: Louisiana's merit-based scholarship program covering tuition at eligible in-state public colleges and universities for qualifying high school graduates.
eligibilitySummary: Louisiana residents who graduate from an eligible high school meeting GPA and ACT score requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Family AGI must not exceed $150,000 for core TOPS awards

name: Louisiana Adult Education
description: Provides free GED preparation, adult basic education, and English as a Second Language programs at locations across Louisiana.
eligibilitySummary: Adults 16 and older who are not enrolled in school and need basic education or GED preparation

name: Total Community Action (New Orleans)
description: New Orleans community action agency providing comprehensive anti-poverty services including energy assistance, Head Start, workforce development, and emergency assistance.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in the greater New Orleans area

name: Catholic Charities Archdiocese of New Orleans
description: Provides comprehensive social services including immigration legal services, disaster recovery, food pantries, counseling, and housing assistance in the New Orleans area.
eligibilitySummary: All individuals and families in need regardless of faith

name: Baton Rouge Area Foundation - Disaster and Community Aid
description: Provides community development, disaster recovery assistance, and grants for nonprofits serving the greater Baton Rouge area.
eligibilitySummary: Residents and nonprofit organizations in the greater Baton Rouge area

name: Louisiana LaCHIP (Children's Health Insurance Program)
description: Louisiana's CHIP program providing affordable health coverage for uninsured children in families earning too much for Medicaid but unable to afford private coverage.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children under 19 in families with income above Medicaid limits but below CHIP thresholds
incomeLimitNotes: Children in families up to 217% FPL

name: Louisiana Lifeline Telephone Assistance
description: Provides discounts on monthly telephone or broadband service for qualifying low-income Louisiana households to maintain access to essential communications.
eligibilitySummary: Households participating in SNAP, Medicaid, SSI, or with income at or below 135% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: 135% FPL or participation in qualifying federal assistance program

name: Louisiana Head Start Association
description: Federally funded early childhood education program promoting school readiness for children from birth to age 5 in low-income Louisiana families.
eligibilitySummary: Families with children birth to age 5 at or below federal poverty guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: 100% FPL or below; foster and homeless children automatically eligible
